title: aspidium tricholepis
intro: Aspidium tricholepis, also known as the Japanese oak fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Japan and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Aspidium tricholepis typically involve drying and processing the fern leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
